data.title: Connecticut's Trusted Home Remodeling Team
data.subtitle: Licensed remodeling contractor serving Fairfield County and New Haven County. We communicate clearly, show up when scheduled, and finish what we start.

data.title: About BuiltWell CT
data.content:
BuiltWell CT was founded by David Megeneishvili, a contractor with nearly 15 years in the restoration industry. David spent years rebuilding homes after fire and water damage, completing full gut renovations from the studs up and working directly with carriers like State Farm, Liberty Mutual, Chubb, and PURE.

Insurance carriers hold contractors to some of the most demanding standards in the industry. David built his career meeting those standards, and that discipline carries directly into how BuiltWell operates today. We handle kitchens, bathrooms, basements, additions, flooring, painting, carpentry, and more across Fairfield County and New Haven County.

Every project starts with an honest assessment. We walk the space, identify what needs to happen structurally and mechanically, and provide a clear scope of work before anything begins. No vague estimates, no surprise change orders, no corners cut to save time.

We communicate before problems become surprises. We keep the schedule we commit to. We complete what we start. Those are not aspirations. They are the standard we hold ourselves to on every project, for every client.

[See our home remodeling services](/services/) or [read our Connecticut remodeling case studies](/case-studies/) to see what that looks like in practice.
data.image_alt: David Megeneishvili, owner of BuiltWell CT, consulting with a homeowner about a home remodeling project outside their Connecticut residence

members.name: David Megeneishvili
members.bio:
David brings nearly 15 years of experience in the restoration and rebuilding industry to every BuiltWell project. Before founding the company, he spent years rebuilding homes after fire damage, water damage, and other disasters, managing full gut renovations from the studs up. He worked directly with major insurance carriers including State Farm, Liberty Mutual, Chubb, PURE, and other national providers, meeting the rigorous documentation, timeline, and quality standards that insurance companies demand. That experience in one of the most scrutinized segments of the construction industry shaped how he runs BuiltWell today.

David built his approach around a simple principle: assess the underlying systems first, address any structural concerns, then build the finish work. This methodology means fewer surprises during construction and better long-term results. He is involved in every project during the consultation and planning phases, ensuring that each proposal accurately reflects the scope of work and that homeowners understand exactly what they are getting.
members.image_alt: David Megeneishvili, owner and founder of BuiltWell CT home remodeling, standing by the company van in Connecticut

members.name: Malkhazi Tvalabeishvili
members.bio:
Malkhazi brings decades of hands-on construction experience to every BuiltWell project. His expertise spans framing, finish carpentry, tile work, and the full range of residential construction trades. As Lead Foreman, he oversees on-site execution and ensures that every project meets BuiltWell's quality standards. His deep knowledge of building systems allows the team to identify and address issues that less experienced contractors might miss.

Known for his meticulous attention to detail and problem-solving abilities, Malkhazi is the person who turns plans into reality. He manages field crews with a steady hand, ensuring that timelines are met without compromising on craftsmanship. Homeowners consistently praise his professionalism, his respect for their property, and his ability to explain complex construction steps in clear, understandable terms.
members.image_alt: Malkhazi Tvalabeishvili, lead foreman at BuiltWell CT, standing in front of BuiltWell service vehicles in Connecticut

data.title: Our Remodeling Process
data.subtitle: We follow the same five-step process on every project, whether it's a single bathroom or a whole-home renovation. It keeps projects on schedule and keeps you informed at every stage.

steps.title: Consultation
steps.description: We visit your home or connect via Google Meet or Zoom at no charge and with no obligation, so we can understand the scope before we put anything in writing.

steps.title: Planning
steps.description: You receive a written proposal with a clear breakdown of exactly what's included, the full project timeline, and the cost. No vague ranges and no surprises later.

steps.title: Selections
steps.description: We guide you through every material choice and communicate lead times up front, so nothing delays construction once the schedule is set.

steps.title: Build
steps.description: Construction begins on the agreed start date. Crews show up on time, daily updates are sent, and the job site is cleaned every evening.

steps.title: Walkthrough
steps.description: When the work is finished, we walk through the project together. Nothing is signed off until you're satisfied with every detail.

data.title: Licensed and Insured in Connecticut
data.content:
We hold CT Home Improvement Contractor License #0668405, issued by the State of Connecticut Department of Consumer Protection. We carry general liability insurance and workers' compensation coverage on every project.

If something goes wrong on your property during construction, you're protected. You're not left dealing with an uninsured contractor and a coverage dispute. Hiring a licensed contractor isn't just a formality. It's how you protect your home and your investment.
